title: White House admits it used keywords to terminate California research grants | AP News
excerpt: Federal agencies that terminated more than a thousand research grants at the University of California last year admitted that they used keywords related to diversity, gender, vaccine hesitancy and COVID-19 to screen for projects that ran afoul of the Trump administration’s priorities.
content_html:
Federal agencies that terminated more than a thousand research grants at the University of California last year admitted that they used keywords related to diversity, gender, vaccine hesitancy and COVID-19 to screen for projects that ran afoul of the Trump administration’s priorities.

It’s an admission that lawyers for the research professors say is proof that the agencies illegally cancelled nearly $2 billion in grants. And that’s a major development in a lawsuit filed by UC researchers against the Trump administration to permanently get their grants back. The researchers’ legal team is now asking the judge in the case to skip a trial and formally declare that the White House repeatedly violated the constitution, including the First Amendment. A court hearing on whether that’ll happen is scheduled for Oct. 20.

The federal agencies made the admission about the keywords in signed stipulations filed in federal court last week in a case called Thakur v. Trump that’s been ongoing since last spring.

The stipulations in the court filing show the agencies “did not terminate any grants based on alleged noncompliance with the terms of the grant” and instead used “general criteria, rather than grant-specific assessment of each award’s compliance, or performance,” the lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote. Lawyers for the UC professors now contend that the admission proves three wide-ranging constitutional violations:

    1. By using keyword searches to review hundreds of grants and terminate them en masse rather than reviewing them individually on their merits, the Trump administration targeted research it found politically disfavorable, which is a First Amendment violation.

    2. In terminating 283 Department of Energy grants from states where a majority voted for Kamala Harris in the 2024 election, and leaving untouched hundreds of grants that went to “red” states, the Trump administration wrongly punished researchers for merely living in states the White House politically opposed.

    3. And by terminating billions of dollars in grants, and never rerouting that money to other grant opportunities, the Trump administration violated the constitution by not spending money in ways Congress specifically required through laws it passed on how research funds should be used.

CalMatters emailed the U.S. Department of Justice, which acts as the legal defense for the federal agencies, on July 16 to ask whether it agrees that the federal grant-making agency stipulations mean the Trump administration violated the constitutional rights of researchers. A spokesperson for the Justice Department didn’t respond by publication time.

History of lawsuit

The judge in this case, Rita F. Lin, has repeatedly sided with the UC professors and issued several preliminary injunctions forcing the federal agencies to restore the grants while she reviews the full merits of the case. This includes the restoration of hundreds of grants at UCLA that the Trump administration sought to suspend over its allegations that the university tolerated antisemitism and permitted transgender women to compete on women’s sports teams.

4 MIN READ

12 MIN READ

4 MIN READ

Those allegations were also the subject of a demand from the White House for UCLA to pay $1.2 billion or experience an additional cascade of research freezes. However, months before Trump sought that settlement UCLA took steps to address antisemitism on campus, including commissioning a task force to recommend ways to create a more welcoming environment for Jewish students.

Lin, a Biden appointee, faulted the administration for disregarding those efforts by UCLA in a preliminary injunction for a different but related lawsuit. The Trump administration’s justification for terminating the grants did not “mention the remedial steps UCLA had already taken to address the issues described,” Lin wrote.

Lawyers for professors in the Thakur case now want Lin to make a final ruling ordering the grants to be restored, rather than a preliminary one. Even if that were to happen, the federal agencies could decide to appeal Lin’s ruling to a higher court. The stakes “are huge,” lawyers for the professors in the Thakur case wrote. If the White House is “allowed to terminate this funding, the researchers, graduate students, and program staff that relied on the grants to pursue their lives’ work will all suffer significant damage to their reputations and careers.”

The lawyers warn of lifesaving cures that will never be developed, new understandings of diseases that will go unexplored, and the decline in the country’s global standing as an international juggernaut in publicly funded research.

Using keywords to target disfavored grants

The agencies admitted to using keywords in various ways, according to a CalMatters review of the new court filings.

The Department of Transportation identified six grants for projects worth about $42 million to terminate because they sought “transportation equity,” prioritized “disadvantaged communities,” or focused on “diversifying the transportation workforce” and “equity.”

Sign up for Morning Wire: Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.

The termination of three of those grants led by a UC Davis professor resulted in 77 researchers abandoning 79 in-progress projects. Lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote that the project leaders were also forced to lay off or scramble to replace funding for more than 40 graduate and undergraduate research assistants.

The National Institutes of Health admitted to using an internal search tool to flag UC grants that mentioned “health equity,” “work force diversity,” “structural racism,” and “sexual orientation.” The NIH alone suspended or cancelled more than 1,000 UC grants, including nearly 700 at UCLA, related to vaccines, cancer research and disparities in health outcomes. That figure is newly disclosed in a court filing — last year, UCLA indicated that closer to 500 NIH grants were terminated. The health research agency also declared that it’s possible that DOGE, the short-lived federal office that billionaire Elon Musk led in 2025, used AI to target grants for termination. Lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote to the judge that these admissions show the agencies “identified the viewpoints they wanted to suppress, searched the grants they funded for those that expressed those viewpoints, and terminated those grants on that basis.” That violates the researchers’ First Amendment rights, the lawyers wrote. The Environmental Protection Agency, one of the named agencies in the suit, is in the process of settling with lawyers for the UC professors to avoid further litigation.

A separate federal district judge in Massachusetts last week dealt the Trump administration a blow in its ability to terminate grants and withhold federal funding. The White House relied on a legal phrase in many of its funding cancellations — “no longer effectuates . . . agency priorities.” California and 22 other states sued, arguing that there’s no law or right for the federal government to cancel funding based on new program goals and White House desires after the grants were already awarded. The judge agreed.

___

This story was originally published by CalMatters and distributed through a partnership with The Associated Press.

——

This version fixes typo in byline.

sentences.zh: 去年终止加州大学一千多项研究资助的联邦机构承认，他们使用与多样性、性别、疫苗犹豫和 COVID-19 相关的关键词来筛选与特朗普政府优先事项相冲突的项目。

blocks.html_en: It’s an admission that lawyers for the research professors say is proof that the agencies illegally cancelled nearly $2 billion in grants. And that’s a major development in a lawsuit filed by UC researchers against the Trump administration to permanently get their grants back. The researchers’ legal team is now asking the judge in the case to skip a trial and formally declare that the White House repeatedly violated the constitution, including the First Amendment. A court hearing on whether that’ll happen is scheduled for Oct. 20.

sentences.en: It’s an admission that lawyers for the research professors say is proof that the agencies illegally cancelled nearly $2 billion in grants.
sentences.zh: 研究教授的律师称，这一承认证明了这些机构非法取消了近 20 亿美元的资助。

sentences.en: And that’s a major development in a lawsuit filed by UC researchers against the Trump administration to permanently get their grants back.
sentences.zh: 这是加州大学研究人员向特朗普政府提起诉讼以永久收回资助的重大进展。

sentences.en: The researchers’ legal team is now asking the judge in the case to skip a trial and formally declare that the White House repeatedly violated the constitution, including the First Amendment.
sentences.zh: 研究人员的法律团队现在要求该案法官跳过审判并正式宣布白宫多次违反宪法，包括第一修正案。

sentences.en: A court hearing on whether that’ll happen is scheduled for Oct. 20.
sentences.zh: 关于是否会发生这种情况的法庭听证会定于 10 月 20 日举行。

sentences.en: Trump that’s been ongoing since last spring.
sentences.zh: 特朗普的这种做法自去年春天以来一直在持续。

sentences.en: The stipulations in the court filing show the agencies “did not terminate any grants based on alleged noncompliance with the terms of the grant” and instead used “general criteria, rather than grant-specific assessment of each award’s compliance, or performance,” the lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote.
sentences.zh: 原告律师写道，法院文件中的规定显示，各机构“并未因涉嫌不遵守资助条款而终止任何资助”，而是使用“一般标准，而不是针对每个资助的合规性或绩效进行具体评估”。

sentences.en: Lawyers for the UC professors now contend that the admission proves three wide-ranging constitutional violations:
sentences.zh: 加州大学教授的律师现在辩称，这一录取证明了三项广泛的违宪行为：

blocks.html_en: 1. By using keyword searches to review hundreds of grants and terminate them en masse rather than reviewing them individually on their merits, the Trump administration targeted research it found politically disfavorable, which is a First Amendment violation.

sentences.en: By using keyword searches to review hundreds of grants and terminate them en masse rather than reviewing them individually on their merits, the Trump administration targeted research it found politically disfavorable, which is a First Amendment violation.
sentences.zh: 通过使用关键词搜索来审查数百项拨款并集体终止它们，而不是根据其优点进行单独审查，特朗普政府将其发现的政治上不利的研究作为目标，这违反了第一修正案。

blocks.html_en: 2. In terminating 283 Department of Energy grants from states where a majority voted for Kamala Harris in the 2024 election, and leaving untouched hundreds of grants that went to “red” states, the Trump administration wrongly punished researchers for merely living in states the White House politically opposed.

sentences.en: In terminating 283 Department of Energy grants from states where a majority voted for Kamala Harris in the 2024 election, and leaving untouched hundreds of grants that went to “red” states, the Trump administration wrongly punished researchers for merely living in states the White House politically opposed.
sentences.zh: 特朗普政府终止了 283 项能源部向在 2024 年大选中多数投票给卡马拉·哈里斯的州提供的拨款，并保留了数百笔向“红色”州提供的拨款，错误地惩罚了仅仅生活在白宫政治反对州的研究人员。

blocks.html_en: 3. And by terminating billions of dollars in grants, and never rerouting that money to other grant opportunities, the Trump administration violated the constitution by not spending money in ways Congress specifically required through laws it passed on how research funds should be used.

sentences.en: And by terminating billions of dollars in grants, and never rerouting that money to other grant opportunities, the Trump administration violated the constitution by not spending money in ways Congress specifically required through laws it passed on how research funds should be used.
sentences.zh: 特朗普政府终止了数十亿美元的拨款，并且从未将这些资金转用于其他拨款机会，违反了宪法，没有按照国会通过的关于如何使用研究资金的法律明确要求的方式花钱。

blocks.html_en: CalMatters emailed the U.S. Department of Justice, which acts as the legal defense for the federal agencies, on July 16 to ask whether it agrees that the federal grant-making agency stipulations mean the Trump administration violated the constitutional rights of researchers. A spokesperson for the Justice Department didn’t respond by publication time.

sentences.en: CalMatters emailed the U.S. Department of Justice, which acts as the legal defense for the federal agencies, on July 16 to ask whether it agrees that the federal grant-making agency stipulations mean the Trump administration violated the constitutional rights of researchers.
sentences.zh: CalMatters 于 7 月 16 日向作为联邦机构法律辩护人的美国司法部发送电子邮件，询问其是否同意联邦拨款机构的规定意味着特朗普政府侵犯了研究人员的宪法权利。

sentences.en: A spokesperson for the Justice Department didn’t respond by publication time.
sentences.zh: 截至发稿时，司法部发言人尚未做出回应。

The health research agency also declared that it’s possible that DOGE, the short-lived federal office that billionaire Elon Musk led in 2025, used AI to target grants for termination. Lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote to the judge that these admissions show the agencies “identified the viewpoints they wanted to suppress, searched the grants they funded for those that expressed those viewpoints, and terminated those grants on that basis.” That violates the researchers’ First Amendment rights, the lawyers wrote. The Environmental Protection Agency, one of the named agencies in the suit, is in the process of settling with lawyers for the UC professors to avoid further litigation.

sentences.en: The National Institutes of Health admitted to using an internal search tool to flag UC grants that mentioned “health equity,” “work force diversity,” “structural racism,” and “sexual orientation.”
sentences.zh: 美国国立卫生研究院承认使用内部搜索工具来标记加州大学的拨款，其中提到“健康公平”、“劳动力多样性”、“结构性种族主义”和“性取向”。

sentences.en: The NIH alone suspended or cancelled more than 1,000 UC grants, including nearly 700 at UCLA, related to vaccines, cancer research and disparities in health outcomes.
sentences.zh: 仅 NIH 就暂停或取消了 1,000 多项与疫苗、癌症研究和健康结果差异相关的加州大学拨款，其中包括加州大学洛杉矶分校的近 700 项拨款。

sentences.en: That figure is newly disclosed in a court filing — last year, UCLA indicated that closer to 500 NIH grants were terminated.
sentences.zh: 这个数字是在一份法庭文件中新披露的——去年，加州大学洛杉矶分校表示，近 500 项 NIH 资助被终止。

sentences.en: The health research agency also declared that it’s possible that DOGE, the short-lived federal office that billionaire Elon Musk led in 2025, used AI to target grants for termination.
sentences.zh: 该健康研究机构还宣称，亿万富翁埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 在 2025 年领导的短暂联邦办公室 DOGE 有可能使用人工智能来确定终止资助的目标。

sentences.en: Lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote to the judge that these admissions show the agencies “identified the viewpoints they wanted to suppress, searched the grants they funded for those that expressed those viewpoints, and terminated those grants on that basis.”
sentences.zh: 原告的律师写信给法官，这些承认表明这些机构“确定了他们想要压制的观点，在他们资助的拨款中寻找那些表达这些观点的人，并据此终止了这些拨款。”

sentences.en: That violates the researchers’ First Amendment rights, the lawyers wrote.
sentences.zh: 律师们写道，这侵犯了研究人员的第一修正案权利。

sentences.en: The Environmental Protection Agency, one of the named agencies in the suit, is in the process of settling with lawyers for the UC professors to avoid further litigation.
sentences.zh: 环境保护局是诉讼中指定的机构之一，正在与加州大学教授的律师达成和解，以避免进一步的诉讼。

blocks.html_en: A separate federal district judge in Massachusetts last week dealt the Trump administration a blow in its ability to terminate grants and withhold federal funding. The White House relied on a legal phrase in many of its funding cancellations — “no longer effectuates . . . agency priorities.” California and 22 other states sued, arguing that there’s no law or right for the federal government to cancel funding based on new program goals and White House desires after the grants were already awarded. The judge agreed.

sentences.en: A separate federal district judge in Massachusetts last week dealt the Trump administration a blow in its ability to terminate grants and withhold federal funding.
sentences.zh: 上周，马萨诸塞州的另一位联邦地区法官对特朗普政府终止拨款和扣留联邦资金的能力造成了打击。

sentences.en: The White House relied on a legal phrase in many of its funding cancellations — “no longer effectuates . . .
sentences.zh: 白宫在许多资金取消中都依赖于一个法律短语——“不再有效……”

sentences.en: agency priorities.”
sentences.zh: 机构优先事项。”

sentences.en: California and 22 other states sued, arguing that there’s no law or right for the federal government to cancel funding based on new program goals and White House desires after the grants were already awarded.
sentences.zh: 加利福尼亚州和其他 22 个州提起诉讼，称联邦政府没有法律或权利在拨款已经授予后根据新的项目目标和白宫的愿望取消资助。

sentences.en: The judge agreed.
sentences.zh: 法官同意了。

blocks.html_en: This story was originally published by CalMatters and distributed through a partnership with The Associated Press.

sentences.en: This story was originally published by CalMatters and distributed through a partnership with The Associated Press.
sentences.zh: 本文最初由 CalMatters 发表，并通过与美联社合作发布。

blocks.html_en: This version fixes typo in byline.

sentences.en: This version fixes typo in byline.
sentences.zh: 此版本修复了署名中的拼写错误。

content_html_zh:
去年终止加州大学一千多项研究资助的联邦机构承认，他们使用与多样性、性别、疫苗犹豫和 COVID-19 相关的关键词来筛选与特朗普政府优先事项相冲突的项目。

研究教授的律师称，这一承认证明了这些机构非法取消了近 20 亿美元的资助。 这是加州大学研究人员向特朗普政府提起诉讼以永久收回资助的重大进展。 研究人员的法律团队现在要求该案法官跳过审判并正式宣布白宫多次违反宪法，包括第一修正案。 关于是否会发生这种情况的法庭听证会定于 10 月 20 日举行。

联邦机构在上周向联邦法院提交的一桩名为“塔库尔诉美国”的案件中承认了已签署规定中的关键词。 特朗普的这种做法自去年春天以来一直在持续。

原告律师写道，法院文件中的规定显示，各机构“并未因涉嫌不遵守资助条款而终止任何资助”，而是使用“一般标准，而不是针对每个资助的合规性或绩效进行具体评估”。 加州大学教授的律师现在辩称，这一录取证明了三项广泛的违宪行为：

    1. 通过使用关键词搜索来审查数百项拨款并集体终止它们，而不是根据其优点进行单独审查，特朗普政府将其发现的政治上不利的研究作为目标，这违反了第一修正案。

    2. 特朗普政府终止了 283 项能源部向在 2024 年大选中多数投票给卡马拉·哈里斯的州提供的拨款，并保留了数百笔向“红色”州提供的拨款，错误地惩罚了仅仅生活在白宫政治反对州的研究人员。

    3. 特朗普政府终止了数十亿美元的拨款，并且从未将这些资金转用于其他拨款机会，违反了宪法，没有按照国会通过的关于如何使用研究资金的法律明确要求的方式花钱。

CalMatters 于 7 月 16 日向作为联邦机构法律辩护人的美国司法部发送电子邮件，询问其是否同意联邦拨款机构的规定意味着特朗普政府侵犯了研究人员的宪法权利。 截至发稿时，司法部发言人尚未做出回应。

诉讼历史

本案的法官 Rita F. 林女士多次站在加州大学教授一边，并发布了几项初步禁令，迫使联邦机构恢复拨款，同时她正在审查案件的全部案情。 这包括恢复加州大学洛杉矶分校的数百项资助，特朗普政府因指控该大学容忍反犹太主义并允许跨性别女性参加女子运动队比赛而试图暂停这些资助。

4 分钟阅读

12 分钟阅读

4 分钟阅读

这些指控也是白宫要求加州大学洛杉矶分校支付 12 亿美元的主题，否则将遭受额外的研究冻结。 然而，在特朗普寻求解决方案的几个月前，加州大学洛杉矶分校采取了措施解决校园内的反犹太主义问题，包括委托成立一个工作组来建议如何为犹太学生创造一个更受欢迎的环境。

拜登任命的林在针对另一起不同但相关诉讼的初步禁令中指责政府无视加州大学洛杉矶分校的这些努力。 林写道，特朗普政府终止拨款的理由并没有“提及加州大学洛杉矶分校为解决所述问题已经采取的补救措施”。

塔库尔案中教授的律师现在希望林做出最终裁决，命令恢复补助金，而不是初步裁决。 即使这种情况发生，联邦机构也可能决定将林的裁决向上级法院提出上诉。 塔库尔案教授的律师写道，赌注“巨大”。 如果白宫“被允许终止这笔资金，那么依赖这笔资金从事毕生工作的研究人员、研究生和项目工作人员的声誉和职业生涯都将遭受重大损害。”

律师们警告说，挽救生命的治疗方法永远不会被开发出来，对疾病的新认识将有待探索，而且该国作为公共资助研究领域国际巨头的全球地位正在下降。

使用关键字来定位不受欢迎的资助

根据 CalMatters 对新法庭文件的审查，这些机构承认以各种方式使用关键词。

交通部确定终止六项价值约 4200 万美元的项目拨款，因为它们寻求“交通公平”，优先考虑“弱势社区”，或专注于“交通劳动力多元化”和“公平”。

订阅 Morning Wire：我们的旗舰时事通讯详细介绍了当天的头条新闻。

由加州大学戴维斯分校教授领导的其中三项资助的终止导致 77 名研究人员放弃了 79 个正在进行的项目。 原告的律师写道，项目负责人还被迫解雇或争先恐后地更换 40 多名研究生和本科生研究助理的资金。

美国国立卫生研究院承认使用内部搜索工具来标记加州大学的拨款，其中提到“健康公平”、“劳动力多样性”、“结构性种族主义”和“性取向”。 仅 NIH 就暂停或取消了 1,000 多项与疫苗、癌症研究和健康结果差异相关的加州大学拨款，其中包括加州大学洛杉矶分校的近 700 项拨款。 这个数字是在一份法庭文件中新披露的——去年，加州大学洛杉矶分校表示，近 500 项 NIH 资助被终止。 该健康研究机构还宣称，亿万富翁埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 在 2025 年领导的短暂联邦办公室 DOGE 有可能使用人工智能来确定终止资助的目标。 原告的律师写信给法官，这些承认表明这些机构“确定了他们想要压制的观点，在他们资助的拨款中寻找那些表达这些观点的人，并据此终止了这些拨款。” 律师们写道，这侵犯了研究人员的第一修正案权利。 环境保护局是诉讼中指定的机构之一，正在与加州大学教授的律师达成和解，以避免进一步的诉讼。

上周，马萨诸塞州的另一位联邦地区法官对特朗普政府终止拨款和扣留联邦资金的能力造成了打击。 白宫在许多资金取消中都依赖于一个法律短语——“不再有效……” 机构优先事项。” 加利福尼亚州和其他 22 个州提起诉讼，称联邦政府没有法律或权利在拨款已经授予后根据新的项目目标和白宫的愿望取消资助。 法官同意了。

___

本文最初由 CalMatters 发表，并通过与美联社合作发布。

——

此版本修复了署名中的拼写错误。
